ogTitle: Longwood East vs Lorquon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Longwood East and Lorquon.
heading: Longwood East vs Lorquon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Longwood East, Victoria and Lorquon, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Longwood East has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Lorquon (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Longwood East or Lorquon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Longwood East has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Lorquon scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Longwood East scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Lorquon scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Longwood East or Lorquon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Longwood East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
